surfaced_from: PWA request 2026-05-25 05:19 UTC — CISSP-like CTO security audit and prioritization
affects: core-cto / public repo / PWA push identity
capability_needed: Rotate and scrub VAPID/Web Push private key leaked into repo history
details: Read-only audit found an EC private key for the PWA VAPID/Web Push identity in git history and a live private.pem present in the working tree. Even if the current file is untracked, the historical private key must be treated as compromised for a public repo. This affects push-message authenticity and is also a marker that binary/runtime artifacts entered source control before the public flip.
search_trail: Ran openclaw status and openclaw security audit --deep on cto-v1; result: 0 critical, 1 warn, 1 info; gateway bound to 127.0.0.1 with token auth.
Ran ss -ltnup on cto-v1; found public SSH plus public HTTP/HTTPS and loopback-only OpenClaw/Hermes/A2A services.
Queried Hetzner API for servers, primary IPs, and firewalls; found no cloud firewalls and multiple public CTO servers/candidates.
Inspected /etc/caddy/Caddyfile without secrets; cto.husband.llc reverse-proxies to 127.0.0.1:8088 with security headers and access logging.
Scanned git history/current tree for secret-shaped patterns and key material; confirmed historical VAPID private key material and multiple documented credential-handling risks.
Checked file permissions for /opt/cto, ~/.openclaw, ~/.hermes, ~/.codex; sensitive home config dirs are mostly 0600/0700, while project files are group-writable by cto group.
Checked unattended-upgrades; service is enabled and active.
Attempted privileged sshd/fail2ban verification; elevated execution is unavailable in this PWA runtime, so privileged SSH posture remains unverified.
proposed_resolution: Rotate the VAPID keypair, update the deployed PWA, invalidate old subscriptions as needed, and scrub the historical private key with git-filter-repo/BFG if John accepts a public-history rewrite.
blast_radius: Existing push subscriptions may need to be re-enrolled; rewriting public history can disrupt clones/forks.
rollback_plan: If rotation breaks push, restore the previous server-side key only temporarily while re-enrolling, then rotate again; if history rewrite is too disruptive, leave history but rotate and document compromise acceptance.
resolution_notes: Runtime VAPID key was rotated on 2026-05-26T20:54Z with old runtime key retained only as an ignored local backup; PWA backend verified serving the new public key. Push re-enrollment/runtime push verification and coordinated public git-history scrub or documented risk acceptance remain open.
